sections.paragraph:
αἱρετός, ή, όν comp. αἱρετώτερος desirable (so Hdt. et al.; LXX; Ath. R. 75, 23; Sb 6996, 25 [II a.d.]) αἱρετώτερον ἦν αὐτοῖς w. inf. foll. (X., Cyr. 3, 3, 51; Diod S 14, 45, 3; Polyaenus 4, 6, 6; Aesop, Fab. 261 P.=273 H.; 168 H-H.; 223 Ch.; cp. Sextus 362) it would have been better for them Hv 4, 2, 6.—DELG s.v. αἱρέω.

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 28
